title: galium vernum var. wirtgenii
intro: Galium vernum var. wirtgenii, also known as Wirtgen's beak-ruby, is a subspecies of the Galium vernum plant, a type of flowering plant in the bedstraw family that is native to Europe. Traditionally, Galium vernum var. wirtgenii has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues, although more research is needed to confirm these claims. Galium vernum var. wirtgenii can be prepared as an herbal remedy in the form of teas, infusions, or tinctures, although its use should be under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
